description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark metal, featuring a slightly flared muzzle and reinforcing rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from dark, textured wood, with visible grain patterns and robust side panels, supporting the heavy barrel. The wheels are solid and wooden, attached to axles that allow the cannon to be repositioned. Metal fittings and brackets secure the barrel to the carriage, and a small metal ring is visible on top for maneuvering or lifting. The overall design is reminiscent of artillery used in the 17th to 19th centuries, intended for use in siege or defensive scenarios.
description: A historical cannon with a long dark metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four wheels and metal fittings. The design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for combat or defensive use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the two visible wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage, the breech of the cannon, and the wheel arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the barrel, carriage, and wheels from the other profile.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the carriage and barrel are exposed with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the object is finished in solid dark colors.
